next_step_text: Confidence 50% 30th Jun Reach out to Wheelson for update/status- start building direct relationship with Mumtalkat We are yet to meet prospect and only demo'd to Wheelson (2 times) and are shortlisted to us and Aerchain - activity has slowed due to geo political issues, expect to resume next week- we MUST take control of cycle moving forward- 3rd June - Spoke to Wheelson CEO, no update from Mumtal

recent_activities_14d.subject: [Clari - Email Sent] Re: Regarding Mumtalakat- Zycus

competitive_position.summary: Live two-horse Enterprise race vs JAGGAER, with Aerchain also on the Enterprise track. Coupa/GEP/iValua/SAP Ariba in the broader field but not active finalists; Zip won the separate Small track. Strongest threat is JAGGAER: capabilities are close, so without direct Mumtalakat validation of the Merlin + supplier-intelligence wedge the decision could revert to price or brand.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain [JAGGAER per SF Competitors field] for the Enterprise project track
competitors.how_we_win: Win on supplier intelligence (iSupplier + iRisk + ANA: internal 12yr data + external intelligence compilation, JAGGAER weak here) and on Merlin AI extensibility (build/orchestrate agents without OEM lock-in). Make supplier intelligence and Merlin the centerpiece of the NDA workshop.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: 5 vendors evaluated, shortlisted to 2 (Zycus and JAGGAER)

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: 5 vendors evaluated, shortlisted to 2 (Zycus and JAGGAER)

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: 5 vendors evaluated, shortlisted to 2 (Zycus and JAGGAER)

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: 5 vendors evaluated, shortlisted to 2 (Zycus and JAGGAER)

competitors.name: Zip (Small track, not competing with Zycus Enterprise)
competitors.quote: Zip shortlisted for the Small project track
competitors.how_we_win: Not a direct competitor; Zip is for Small track, Zycus is Enterprise track.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.source: Next Step 15 Apr 2026; MEDDPICC CRM 15 May 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Not competing with Zycus; Zip is on a separate Small/mid-market track while Zycus is on the Enterprise track.

competitors.name: Aerchain
competitors.quote: Zycus has been shortlisted for phase 2 alongside Aerchain for the 'Enterprise' project track.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus end-to-end S2P depth and build-your-own Merlin agents against a narrower new-age point player.

champion_strength.summary: Samynathan R. (Wheelson CEO) is an active, well-informed partner champion who arranged the intro, researched Merlin, and authored the evaluation structure, but he is a consultancy champion, not an internal Mumtalakat advocate — both the deal's strength and its single point of failure.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Mumtalakat budget owner is identified by name. MEDDPICC 2.0 records the budget owner as 'Mumtalakat' generically and explicitly states the economic buyer is TBC, with the Wheelson CEO as the key contact representing the consultancy, not the end client (source: MEDDPICC 2.0). All engagement flows through Wheelson; Zycus has never met the prospect directly (source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-30). As a sovereign wealth fund, a paid POC and 60-entity rollout likely require investment-committee or board approval whose chain and thresholds are unknown. The path to power runs THROUGH the champion — arming Samynathan to broker the Mumtalakat sponsor is the top move, not cold-outreach into the end client.

recommended_moves: Ask Samynathan to broker a direct intro to the Mumtalakat procurement or pilot-entity sponsor — position it as joint Wheelson-Zycus stage-2 prep, not a bypass.
Agree the stage-2 restart trigger with Wheelson and pre-stage the NDA workshop + paid-POC scope in writing (including who owns it on the Zycus side).
Pre-build the Merlin + external-supplier-intelligence differentiation pack versus JAGGAER/Aerchain so Zycus is workshop-ready the moment stage 2 reopens.
